sectionOrderRules: Hero must communicate the type of travel or experiences offered and include a primary booking or contact CTA.
Trip-highlights should show concrete destinations, themes or packages rather than vague promises.
Trust-proof must reference only real trust signals such as resegaranti, applicable travel law, clear booking terms or authentic customer reviews when available.
Gallery-preview, pricing-preview and reviews may appear between trip-highlights and trust-proof when data exists.
Avoid repeating the same destination or sales argument across multiple adjacent sections.
Trips intro must explain what kinds of resor, destinationer or upplägg the business actually offers.
Trip-list items must be concrete and may include destination, duration, target audience, season, included highlights or travel style when known.
If reviews are shown, they should support the listed trips without inventing outcomes or quantities.
Pricing intro must clarify whether prices are examples, starting prices or fixed package prices when that information is available.
Pricing-table must focus on packages, tiers or inclusions relevant to travel booking and avoid unclear placeholder pricing.
If trust-proof is shown here, it should reinforce clarity around what is included, payment terms or traveler protections without duplicating the exact same block from the home page.
Booking-intro must explain how booking works, for example request flow, consultation, confirmation or online booking step.
Booking-options should present the actual available booking path such as form, phone, email or scheduled consultation.
Do not imply instant booking confirmation unless the business actually supports it.
Contact-info must include real available contact methods and must not use placeholder details.
If opening hours are shown, they should reflect customer contact or advisory availability rather than implying staffed travel support around the clock.
Map is optional and should be shown only when a visitable address is relevant.
